entities.name: tirzepatide

attributes.drug_class: dual GIP and GLP-1 receptor agonist

entities.context: tirzepatide, a novel glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide and glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist, in people with obesity are not known.

entities.name: Obesity

attributes.description: chronic disease resulting in substantial global morbidity and mortality
attributes.eligibility_criteria: BMI >= 30, or BMI >= 27 with at least one weight-related complication, excluding diabetes

entities.context: Obesity is a chronic disease that results in substantial global morbidity and mortality.

entities.name: GIP receptor agonism
entities.context: tirzepatide, a novel glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide and glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist

entities.context: tirzepatide, a novel glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide and glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist

entities.name: SURMOUNT-1

attributes.primary_endpoint: percentage change in weight from baseline; proportion achieving >= 5% weight reduction
attributes.patient_population: Adults with BMI >= 30, or BMI >= 27 with at least one weight-related complication, excluding diabetes

entities.context: Supported by Eli Lilly; SURMOUNT-1 ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT04184622.

entities.name: Eli Lilly
entities.context: Supported by Eli Lilly; SURMOUNT-1 ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT04184622.

entities.name: Gastrointestinal Adverse Events

attributes.frequency: most common adverse events with tirzepatide

entities.context: The most common adverse events with tirzepatide were gastrointestinal, and most were mild to moderate in severity, occurring primarily during dose escalation.

entities.name: Treatment Discontinuation due to Adverse Events
entities.context: Adverse events caused treatment discontinuation in 4.3%, 7.1%, 6.2%, and 2.6% of participants receiving 5-mg, 10-mg, and 15-mg tirzepatide doses and placebo, respectively.

entities.name: Jastreboff et al., NEJM 2022

attributes.journal: The New England Journal of Medicine

entities.context: Title: Tirzepatide Once Weekly for the Treatment of Obesity. Journal: The New England journal of medicine (2022). PMID: 35658024.

entities.name: body weight reduction >= 5%

attributes.clinical_relevance: primary endpoint for obesity treatment efficacy

entities.context: Coprimary end points were the percentage change in weight from baseline and a weight reduction of 5% or more.

entities.name: body weight reduction >= 20%

attributes.clinical_relevance: secondary efficacy endpoint for obesity treatment

entities.context: 50% (95% CI, 46 to 54) and 57% (95% CI, 53 to 61) of participants in the 10-mg and 15-mg groups had a reduction in body weight of 20% or more, as compared with 3% (95% CI, 1 to 5) in the placebo group.

entities.name: cardiometabolic risk factors
entities.context: Improvements in all prespecified cardiometabolic measures were observed with tirzepatide.

relations.evidence: In this 72-week trial in participants with obesity, 5 mg, 10 mg, or 15 mg of tirzepatide once weekly provided substantial and sustained reductions in body weight.
